member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig
member.summary: Represents configuration options for performing bulk data operations.
member.remarks: Use this class to specify settings such as batch size, identity preservation, locking behavior, and data handling when executing bulk insert or update operations. These options allow customization of performance, transactional behavior, and data mapping during bulk processing.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the BulkConfig class with default configuration settings.
member.remarks: The default values are: WithHoldlock is set to , KeepIdentity is set to , and BatchSize is set to 0. These defaults can be changed by setting the corresponding properties after instantiation.

member.name: P:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig.BatchSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of items to process in each batch operation.

member.name: P:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig.WithHoldlock
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the HOLDLOCK table hint is applied to queries.
member.remarks: When set to , queries use the HOLDLOCK hint to hold shared locks until the end of the transaction, which can help prevent certain concurrency issues. Set to to omit the HOLDLOCK hint. The default value is .

member.name: P:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig.KeepIdentity
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to preserve identity values when inserting data.
member.remarks: Set this property to to retain original identity values during data operations, such as bulk inserts. When set to , new identity values may be generated by the database.

member.name: P:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig.dataHandler
member.summary: Gets or sets the SQL data handler used to perform database operations.
member.remarks: Assign an implementation of ISqlDataHandler to customize how database interactions are handled. This property enables dependency injection for database access logic.

member.name: P:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig.BulkCopyTimeout
member.summary: Gets or sets the time, in seconds, before a bulk copy operation times out.
member.remarks: Set this property to control how long the bulk copy operation is allowed to run before being terminated. A value of 0 indicates no timeout, and the operation will wait indefinitely. Adjust this value based on expected data size and network conditions to avoid premature termination of long-running operations.

member.name: P:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig.NotifyAfter
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of operations to process before triggering a notification.
member.remarks: Set this property to control how frequently notifications are sent during batch processing. A value of 0 or less disables notifications.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.DatabaseType
member.summary: Specifies the supported types of database engines.
member.remarks: Use this enumeration to indicate which database provider is being targeted when configuring database connections or operations. The values correspond to commonly used relational database systems.

member.name: F:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.DatabaseType.SqlServer
member.summary: Specifies the SQL Server database provider.

member.name: F:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.DatabaseType.PostgreSql
member.summary: Represents the PostgreSQL database provider or connection type.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.Formatter.ITableDetailsInstance
member.summary: Defines a contract for generating detailed table information for a collection of entities within a specified database context.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.Formatter.ITableDetailsInstance.GenerateInstance``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Generates a new instance of the table details for the specified entities within the given database context.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities for which to generate table details.
member.param: The database context that provides access to the underlying data store. Cannot be null.
The collection of entities to include in the table details. Cannot be null.
member.returns: A TableDetails object representing the schema and data information for the specified entities.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.Models.TableService
member.summary: Provides utility methods for converting entity collections to DataTable instances using Entity Framework Core metadata.
member.remarks: The TableService class is designed to facilitate interoperability between Entity Framework Core entities and ADO.NET DataTable objects. It ensures that column names and types in the resulting DataTable reflect EF Core configurations, including [Column] attributes and custom mappings. This is useful for scenarios where tabular data representation is required, such as exporting data or integrating with APIs that consume DataTable objects.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.Models.TableService.ConvertToDataTable``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails)
member.summary: Converts entities to DataTable using database column names from EF Core metadata. Respects [Column] attributes and other EF Core column name configurations.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails
member.summary: Represents metadata and mapping details for a database table, including schema, table name, primary keys, property-to-column mappings, and related entity information.
member.remarks: This class is typically used to facilitate dynamic table operations, such as bulk inserts or schema introspection, by providing access to both .NET type information and Entity Framework Core metadata. It supports scenarios where table structure and mappings need to be determined at runtime. Thread safety is not guaranteed; instances are intended for use within a single operation or context.

member.name: P:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails.ColumnMappings
member.summary: Maps C# property names to database column names. Key: Property name (e.g., "UserId") Value: Column name (e.g., "user_id")

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails.BuildColumnMappings(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Metadata.IEntityType,System.Reflection.PropertyInfo[])
member.summary: Builds a mapping of C# property names to database column names using EF Core metadata. This respects [Column] attributes and other EF Core configurations.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails.GetPrimaryKeyInfo(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Metadata.IEntityType)
member.summary: Gets primary key information including both property names and column names.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandler.ISqlDataHandler
member.summary: Defines methods for performing bulk data operations on a SQL database using an Entity Framework Core context.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide efficient mechanisms for inserting, updating, or upserting large collections of entities in a SQL database. These operations are typically optimized for performance and are intended for scenarios where processing large volumes of data is required. Thread safety and transaction management depend on the specific implementation.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandler.ISqlDataHandler.BulkInsertAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously inserts a collection of entities into the database in bulk, using the specified configuration options.
member.remarks: Bulk insert operations can significantly improve performance when adding large numbers of entities compared to individual inserts. The operation is performed within the context's current transaction, if one exists.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to insert. Must be a class that is mapped by the provided DbContext.
member.param: The DbContext instance used to access the database. Cannot be null.
The collection of entities to insert. Cannot be null or contain null elements.
The configuration options for the bulk insert operation. If null, default options are used.
A cancellation token that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous bulk insert operation.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandler.ISqlDataHandler.BulkUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Performs a bulk update operation asynchronously for the specified entities in the given DbContext.
member.remarks: This method is optimized for updating large numbers of entities efficiently. The operation is performed asynchronously and may improve performance compared to updating entities individually. Ensure that the entities are attached to the provided DbContext and that any required configuration is specified in the bulkConfig parameter.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to update. Must be a class that is tracked by the DbContext.
member.param: The DbContext instance used to track and update the entities. Cannot be null.
The collection of entities to update in bulk. Cannot be null or contain null elements.
An optional configuration object that specifies bulk operation options. If null, default settings are used.
A CancellationToken that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous bulk update operation.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandler.ISqlDataHandler.BulkInsertOrUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Performs a bulk insert or update operation for the specified entities in the given DbContext asynchronously.
member.remarks: This method efficiently inserts new entities or updates existing ones in bulk, which can significantly improve performance compared to individual operations. The behavior of the operation can be customized using the bulkConfig parameter. The method does not track changes to the entities after the operation completes.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to insert or update. Must be a class that is mapped in the DbContext.
member.param: The DbContext instance used to perform the bulk operation. Cannot be null.
The collection of entities to insert or update. Cannot be null or empty.
An optional configuration object that specifies options for the bulk operation. If null, default settings are used.
A CancellationToken that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous bulk insert or update operation.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandlerFactory
member.summary: Provides factory methods for creating SQL data handler instances based on the database provider used by a given Entity Framework Core context.
member.remarks: This class supports dynamic creation of data handlers for different database providers, such as SQL Server and PostgreSQL, by inspecting the specified DbContext. It is intended for use in scenarios where bulk operations or provider-specific data handling are required. The factory methods throw exceptions if the database provider is not supported.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandlerFactory.CreateDataHandler(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext)
member.summary: Creates an instance of an SQL data handler that is compatible with the database provider used by the specified Entity Framework context.
member.remarks: Currently supports SQL Server and PostgreSQL database providers. The method uses reflection to load the appropriate handler implementation based on the context's provider. Ensure that the required assemblies are available at runtime.
member.param: The Entity Framework database context for which to create a data handler. The context's provider determines the type of data handler returned. Cannot be null.
member.returns: An implementation of ISqlDataHandler that supports the database provider associated with the specified context.
member.exception: Thrown if the database provider is not supported, if the required handler type cannot be found or instantiated, or if an error occurs during handler creation.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandlerFactory.GetDatabaseType(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext)
member.summary: Determines the type of database provider used by the specified Entity Framework Core context.
member.remarks: Currently supports SQL Server and PostgreSQL providers. Additional providers may require updates to this method.
member.param: The Entity Framework Core database context for which to determine the database provider type. Cannot be null.
member.returns: A value of the DatabaseType enumeration that represents the type of database provider used by the context.
member.exception: Thrown if the database provider used by the context is not supported.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkTransactionManager
member.summary: Provides static methods for performing bulk insert, update, and insert-or-update operations on collections of entities using Entity Framework Core contexts and DbSets. These methods enable efficient processing of large data sets by leveraging optimized bulk operations.
member.remarks: Bulk operations performed by this class are designed to improve performance when handling large numbers of entities, compared to executing individual insert or update commands. The methods support asynchronous execution and allow customization through bulk configuration options. Thread safety and transaction management depend on the underlying DbContext and configuration provided. For best results, ensure that the entities and configuration options are compatible with the database provider in use.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkTransactionManager.BulkInsertAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: This method performs a bulk insert operation for a collection of entities into the database using the provided DbContext.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkTransactionManager.BulkInsertAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbSet{``0},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: This method performs a bulk insert operation for a collection of entities into the database using the provided DbSet.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkTransactionManager.BulkUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: This method performs a bulk update operation for a collection of entities in the database using the provided DbContext.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkTransactionManager.BulkUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbSet{``0},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: This method performs a bulk update operation for a collection of entities in the database using the provided DbSet.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkTransactionManager.BulkInsertOrUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: This method performs a bulk insert or update operation for a collection of entities in the database using the provided DbContext.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkTransactionManager.BulkInsertOrUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbSet{``0},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Performs a bulk insert or update operation for the specified entities in the database asynchronously.
member.remarks: This method uses a bulk operation to efficiently insert new entities or update existing ones in the database. It is suitable for scenarios where large numbers of records need to be processed with improved performance compared to individual insert or update operations.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities in the DbSet. Must be a reference type.
member.param: The DbSet representing the table in which to insert or update entities.
The collection of entities to be inserted or updated. Cannot be null.
The configuration options for the bulk operation. If null, default settings are used.
A token to monitor for cancellation requests. The default value is None.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous bulk insert or update operation.
